locations.name: Keflavik Airport
locations.desc: Your gateway to Iceland. Pick up the rental car here; it is about a 45-minute drive into Reykjavik.

locations.name: NORR Apartment, Reykjavik
locations.desc: Check in to your central base. No sightseeing today - settle in, rest, and grab a relaxed first dinner.

locations.name: Hallgrimskirkja
locations.desc: Iceland's iconic 74 m basalt-column church. Ride the tower lift for the best rooftop view over the colourful city.

locations.name: Sun Voyager
locations.desc: A gleaming steel sculpture of a Viking-style boat by the sea. A symbol of hope and discovery, lovely at low light.

locations.name: Harpa
locations.desc: Striking glass concert hall with a honeycomb facade inspired by basalt. Free to wander inside the shimmering foyer.

locations.name: Tjornin
locations.desc: The central city pond, full of birdlife and ringed by pretty houses. A calm spot in the middle of downtown.

locations.name: Perlan
locations.desc: Glass-dome museum on water tanks with an ice-cave exhibit and 360 degree viewing deck. Great rainy-day option.

locations.name: Phallological Museum
locations.desc: The world's largest collection of mammal phalluses - quirky and unforgettable. A fun only-in-Iceland curiosity.

locations.name: Baejarins Beztu Pylsur
locations.desc: Legendary hot-dog stand serving Iceland's favourite snack since 1937. Order 'eina með öllu' - one with everything.

locations.name: Thorufoss
locations.desc: A pretty, less-crowded waterfall that featured in Game of Thrones. A nice warm-up stop on the way east.

locations.name: Bruarfoss
locations.desc: Famous for its vivid turquoise-blue cascades. Reached by a riverside walk that keeps the crowds thin.

locations.name: Strokkur
locations.desc: Iceland's most reliable geyser, erupting 15-20 m every few minutes. Stand upwind and have your camera ready.

locations.name: Gullfoss
locations.desc: The thundering 'Golden Falls' drops in two tiers into a rugged canyon. One of Iceland's most powerful waterfalls.

locations.name: Kerid
locations.desc: A 3,000-year-old volcanic crater with a striking red rim and an aquamarine lake. Easy walk around the rim.

locations.name: Secret Lagoon
locations.desc: Iceland's oldest natural geothermal pool in Fluðir. A steamy, rustic place to soak after a day of sightseeing.

locations.name: Bridge Between Continents
locations.desc: Walk over a footbridge spanning the rift between the North American and Eurasian plates. A literal step between continents.

locations.name: Gunnuhver
locations.desc: Iceland's largest mud pool, hissing with steam and boiling clay. Named after an angry ghost said to be trapped here.

locations.name: Valahnukur
locations.desc: Dramatic sea cliffs pounded by Atlantic waves, with a nearby lighthouse. Wild, windswept and very photogenic.

locations.name: Krysuvikurberg
locations.desc: Towering bird cliffs alive with seabirds in summer. A quieter, rugged stretch of coastline.

locations.name: Akrafjall
locations.desc: A flat-topped mountain with a parking spot and panoramic fjord views. A quick leg-stretch on the drive north.

locations.name: Grabrok Craters
locations.desc: A young volcanic crater with a short stairway to the rim. Easy 20-minute climb for sweeping lava-field views.

locations.name: Reykholt Viking House
locations.desc: Historic settlement linked to saga-writer Snorri Sturluson. A glimpse into medieval Icelandic culture.

locations.name: Eldborg Crater
locations.desc: A near-perfect ring-shaped scoria crater rising from green lava fields. Striking even seen from the road.

locations.name: Landbrotalaug
locations.desc: A tiny wild hot spring just big enough for two. A magical, secluded soak near your apartment.

locations.name: Eidhus Apartments
locations.desc: Your countryside base on the south side of Snaefellsnes for the next two nights.

locations.name: Ytri-Tunga
locations.desc: A golden-sand beach that is one of the best places in Iceland to see wild seals. Bring binoculars.

locations.name: Arnarstapi
locations.desc: A fishing hamlet with arches, blowholes and basalt sea cliffs. A scenic coastal trail leads toward Hellnar.

locations.name: Vatnshellir Cave
locations.desc: An 8,000-year-old lava tube explored on a guided tour down a spiral staircase. A cool peek underground.

locations.name: Djupalonssandur
locations.desc: A black-pebble beach with dramatic rock formations and old lifting stones once used to test fishermen's strength.

locations.name: Fjoruhusio Cafe
locations.desc: A cosy seaside cafe in Hellnar famous for its fish soup and view. A perfect mid-loop lunch.

locations.name: Kirkjufell and Kirkjufellsfoss
locations.desc: Iceland's most photographed mountain, an elegant cone beside a tumbling waterfall. Postcard-perfect in any weather.

locations.name: South Coast Panorama
locations.desc: Your new base near Hvolsvollur, well placed for the south-coast sights. Check in after the long drive south.

locations.name: Seljalandsfoss
locations.desc: A 60 m waterfall you can walk completely behind. Bring a rain jacket - you will get misted.

locations.name: Gljufrabui
locations.desc: A hidden waterfall tucked inside a mossy canyon, reached by wading a small stream. A secret right next to Seljalandsfoss.

locations.name: Lomagnupur
locations.desc: A sheer 670 m cliff-mountain rising from the sandar plains. One of the most imposing sights on the Ring Road.

locations.name: Fjadrargljufur
locations.desc: A serpentine 100 m-deep canyon with mossy walls and a winding river. A short clifftop trail offers epic views.

locations.name: Systrakaffi
locations.desc: A friendly restaurant in Kirkjubaejarklaustur. A good lunch stop roughly halfway on this long day.

locations.name: Skaftafell and Svartifoss
locations.desc: A waterfall framed by black basalt columns within Vatnajokull National Park. A scenic 1.5 km hike each way.

locations.name: Svinafellsjokull
locations.desc: A crevassed glacier tongue you can view up close from the parking area. Featured in many films.

locations.name: Jokulsarlon
locations.desc: A glacier lagoon dotted with floating icebergs and seals. Iceland's most surreal and beautiful natural sight.

locations.name: Diamond Beach
locations.desc: Black sand strewn with glittering icebergs washed up from the lagoon. Your eastern turnaround point.

locations.name: Skogafoss
locations.desc: A massive 60 m curtain of water with frequent rainbows. Climb the stairs beside it for a top-down view.

locations.name: Kvernufoss
locations.desc: A quieter neighbour of Skogafoss hidden in a green gorge. You can walk behind this one too.

locations.name: Seljavallalaug
locations.desc: One of Iceland's oldest swimming pools, tucked into a mountain valley. A rustic, atmospheric warm soak.

locations.name: Eyjafjallajokull Viewpoint
locations.desc: The infamous volcano whose 2010 eruption grounded European flights. Stop to admire the glacier-capped peak.

locations.name: Dyholaey
locations.desc: A rocky promontory with a giant sea arch and a lighthouse. A prime spot for puffins in summer.

locations.name: Vik and Yoda Cave
locations.desc: The charming southernmost village, near black-sand beaches and the Yoda-shaped Hjorleifshofoi cave. Last stop before heading west.
